steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content:
The subject is Mathematician Grigori Perelman — singular.

The phrase sometimes in conjunction with mathematicians Richard S.

Hamilton and Shing-Tung Yau is a parenthetical interrupter set off by commas; it doesn't change the subject. (Phrases beginning with with, along with, etc., do not affect subject-verb agreement.)

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
A singular subject takes a singular verb. Is credited agrees with Perelman.

Are, are being, and have been are all plural verb forms.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
